title: Targeted Expansion Sequencing Protocols
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"76t8c","text":"This protocol collection accompanies accompanies Expansion Sequencing (ExSeq), covering the four key steps of a targeted Expansion Sequencing (targeted ExSeq) experiment: (1) Padlock probe design; (2) tissue preparation and expansion; (3) library preparation; and (4) in situ sequencing with the Illumina chemistry.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":268,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":49,"length":20}],"data":[]},{"key":"aqeh9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5bkga","text":"For further details, consult the relevant protocols within the collection. These protocols were used to profile human metastatic breast cancer biopsies as a part of the Human Tumor Atlas Pilot Project (HTAPP).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"9A28806BD90D11EBA3C50A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33509999/"}}}}
creator: Anubhav Sinha
